feat_desc_0:
This feature measures your heart rate throughout the day, whether you are at rest, walking, or training. It helps you understand how your heart reacts to effort and recovery.

You can see intensity zones (low, medium, high) to know if you are training too gently or too hard. After a session, the watch indicates if your heart returns quickly to normal, which can show good fitness or, conversely, a need for rest.

It can also send alerts if your heart rate is abnormally high or low when you are not making any effort.

Goal: to guide you to better manage intensity, avoid overwork, and improve your condition safely.
feat_desc_1:
SpO₂ represents the oxygen level in the blood. This measurement is useful for tracking your breathing and overall fitness. The watch can perform spot measurements or over certain periods (for example, at night).

If your SpO₂ drops, it may indicate fatigue, less restorative sleep, or difficult adaptation to effort. This feature primarily helps you spot trends: is it stable? Does it drop after workouts?

You get a simple display (value + status) and sometimes advice: recover better, breathe correctly, avoid intense effort if your body already seems tired.

Goal: to better understand your respiratory state and make better decisions for your sport and recovery.
feat_desc_2:
This feature records your workouts: type of activity, duration, distance (if applicable), estimated calories, heart rate during effort, and sometimes cadence or steps.

After each session, you receive a clear summary: what you did, the intensity, and how your body reacted. You can also track your progress over the week or month: consistency, performance improvement, and training load evolution.

It helps you stay motivated thanks to goals (e.g. number of sessions per week, activity minutes, calories) and easy-to-read statistics.

Goal: to keep a complete history of your sport and allow you to progress in an organized way, without forgetting your sessions.
feat_desc_3:
GPS allows the watch to track your routes during outings (running, walking, cycling). You can see a map of the path and simple data: distance, average speed, total time, sometimes elevation.

This feature is useful for analyzing your performance based on the route: are you faster on a specific road? Does your pace drop on climbs?

It also allows for more precise tracking without needing your phone, depending on the model and settings.

For safety, it can help locate an activity or record the location of a session.

Goal: to measure your outings reliably, compare your performances, and make your outdoor training more precise and intelligent.
feat_desc_4:
This feature helps estimate your stress level and mental fatigue from signals like heart rate, heart rate variability (HRV), and daily activity.

The result is presented simply: a state (calm, normal, stressed) and a trend (improving or degrading). It can suggest easy actions: guided breathing, mini-breaks, earlier sleep, or lighter training if you are too tense.

This is not a medical diagnosis: it is a guidance tool to better manage your mental energy.

Goal: to help you avoid burnout, better plan your days, and improve your performances by keeping a good balance between effort, work, rest, and recovery.
feat_desc_5:
This feature evaluates your overall physical state: fatigue, recovery, and fitness level. It combines several data points (activity, sleep, heart rate, training load) to produce simple indicators.

For example, it can tell you: “Today you are ready for an intense session” or “Today, prioritize recovery”. It helps you avoid training too hard when your body is not ready.

You can track your progress with trends: improved endurance, better recovery, or signs of overwork if fatigue increases too much.

Goal: to adapt your training at the right time, reduce injury risks, and progress faster by respecting your body.
feat_desc_6:
AI coaching creates a program adapted to your goal: lose weight, improve endurance, gain strength, prepare for a competition, or simply stay in shape.

You answer a few details (level, available time, goal), then the watch proposes a clear plan: sessions, days, intensity, and recovery time.

Most importantly: the plan adjusts automatically. If you missed a session, if you are tired, or if your performances progress quickly, the program changes to remain realistic and effective.

Goal: to guide you like a personal coach, with simple recommendations, regular follow-up, and intelligent adjustments to maintain continuous progression without exhausting you.
feat_desc_7:
This feature helps you better manage your diet and water daily. It can suggest hydration reminders adapted to your activity and body heat (based on available data).

For nutrition, it can guide you with simple tips: balancing meals, improving energy before a session, recovering after effort, or avoiding certain errors (bad timing, lack of water).

The goal is not to complicate: it is to give small actions that are easy to follow, with clear tracking (e.g. “water goal achieved”, “good choices today”).

Goal: to support your performance and health through better habits, without a strict diet, just with practical and regular recommendations.
feat_desc_8:
The intelligent medical report groups your health data into a simple reading: heart rate evolution, sleep, stress, SpO₂, activity, and recovery.

Instead of leaving you with numbers, it produces a clear synthesis: what is stable, what is improving, and what may require more rest or attention.

It can also display simple alerts (e.g. “high fatigue for several days”, “drop in sleep”) to push you to correct your habits.

This report is not a diagnosis, but a useful support for understanding your body. It can help if you wish to share a general trend with a coach or health professional.

Goal: to transform your data into easy decisions to improve your health and performance.
feat_0_desc: Heart rate measurement + intensity zones for effort and recovery.
feat_1_desc: Oxygenation tracking for fitness, sleep and effort adaptation.
feat_2_desc: Daily tracking: sessions, calories, progress and sports goals.
feat_3_desc: Location, routes and performance analysis based on the course.
feat_4_desc: Stress / concentration / mental fatigue detection via physiological indicators.
feat_5_desc: Advanced evaluation of performance, recovery and condition.
feat_6_desc: Personalized programs with dynamic adjustment based on your progress.

feat_8_desc: Synthesized general health overview: trends, alerts, and medical follow-up.
